expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: In result.md section "1) Phase 1 Objective (per benchmark focus)" it explicitly lists the three required focus areas: "1. Component-stack constraints ... 2. Embedding lifecycle assumptions ... 3. Integration-sensitive customer expectations". It then provides a "Context Intake Skeleton" with subsections A/B/C corresponding to those areas, preserving them as required intake fields (even though values are marked unknown due to lack of admissible evidence).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The document is explicitly titled "QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 1 (Context Intake)" and states "Primary phase under test: phase1". In "4) Phase Contract Assessment (Phase 1 alignment)" it says "Aligned with phase1? Yes — it is strictly a context intake artifact and does not proceed into test design/execution." The content stays within intake/checks/blockers and does not include test plans or execution steps.
